title: Scheme Source
documentation:
## Purpose
Carries Scheme source files, a minimalist dialect of Lisp characterized by lexical scoping, first-class continuations, and tail-call optimization. Scheme is widely used in academic and research contexts as well as embedded scripting.

## When to use
Use `media:scheme;textable;code` when a pipeline step processes `.scm` or `.ss` source — for example, a REPL evaluation node or a macro-expansion analysis tool.

## Notes
Multiple Scheme implementations (Guile, Racket in compatibility mode, Chicken, Chez) share the `.scm` extension but may differ in available libraries and syntax extensions. See also [Scheme Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:scheme;textable;code).
